evals.prompt: Run an OKR cascade for a B2B analytics SaaS company. Company objectives: (1) Increase enterprise revenue by 40% this year (KR: close 5 new enterprise contracts; KR: expand 3 existing accounts by 50%). (2) Become the trusted analytics partner for mid-market finance teams (KR: NPS from finance users > 50; KR: 80% of users complete key workflow in under 10 minutes). No current OKR cascade exists.
evals.expected_output: An okr-cascade.md artifact committed to the resolved layout path with frontmatter type: okr-cascade. The artifact covers: company OKRs (verbatim), team-level OKRs derived from each company objective (measurable at team scope), a gap registry naming each unowned or under-resourced gap with a kebab-case slug. The causal metric tree is derived: a north-star metric (user-behavioral, outcome-oriented — not 'features shipped'), 2–4 leading indicators that causally predict the north-star (each connected to a specific gap or KR), and the causal chain named explicitly. Gap entries are ranked by OKR weight. Workspace.toml shaping-queue entries are prepared for the highest-priority gaps. The resolved path is surfaced before writing.
assertions: Company OKRs appear verbatim — not interpreted or reworded
Team-level OKRs are derived for each company Objective and are measurable at team scope
Gap registry names at least two gaps with kebab-case slugs; each gap names which KR it is blocking
North-star metric is named: a user-behavioral outcome metric (not an output metric like 'features shipped' or 'MRR')
2–4 leading indicators are named, each with an explicit causal connection to the north-star
Each leading indicator is connected to a specific gap or KR — not a free-floating metric
Gap entries are ranked by OKR weight or strategic priority — not a flat unordered list
Workspace.toml shaping-queue entries are prepared for the highest-priority gaps
The artifact is committed to the resolved path with frontmatter type: okr-cascade; path is surfaced before writing
